explanation: This command simulates a suspicious command line that might trigger the Sigma rule. It uses `cmd.exe` with `/c` to execute a benign command, which aligns with the `CommandLine|contains: 'cmd /c'` detection logic.

This command simulates a suspicious PowerShell invocation using `Invoke-`, which matches the `CommandLine|contains: 'Invoke-'` detection logic in the Sigma rule. The command is benign but structured to test detection coverage.

This command simulates a suspicious use of `rundll32.exe`, which is included in the `Image|endswith` list of suspicious binaries in the Sigma rule. The command is benign but structured to test detection coverage.

This command simulates a benign invocation of `mshta.exe`, which is included in the `Image|endswith` list of suspicious binaries in the Sigma rule. It is used here to test detection coverage without malicious intent.

This command simulates a benign invocation of `cscript.exe`, which is included in the `Image|endswith` list of suspicious binaries in the Sigma rule. It is used here to test detection coverage without malicious intent.
